stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. Construction begins with site planning, orientation and hazard assessment.
stages.explanation: Construction begins with site planning, orientation and hazard assessment.

stages.title: Setting Out
stages.narration: Stage 2. Setting out. The building grid and wall centerlines are established on site.
stages.explanation: Accurate setting out ensures correct alignment of interlocking blocks.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Excavation. Foundation trenches are cut to competent bearing strata.
stages.explanation: Excavation reaches competent bearing strata capable of supporting structural loads.

stages.title: PCC Layer
stages.narration: Stage 4. Plain cement concrete is placed to level and protect the trench base.
stages.explanation: PCC provides a stable working surface and prevents contamination of structural concrete.

stages.title: RCC Footings
stages.narration: Stage 5. Reinforcement cages are assembled and footings are cast in reinforced concrete.
stages.explanation: Footings safely distribute building loads into the soil.

stages.title: Plinth Band
stages.narration: Stage 6. The reinforced concrete plinth band is cast continuously around the structure.
stages.explanation: The plinth band improves continuity and controls differential settlement.

stages.title: First Interlocking Block Course
stages.narration: Stage 7. The first course of interlocking hollow blocks is laid. Blocks lock together automatically.
stages.explanation: Interlocking geometry ensures alignment and reduces construction errors.

stages.title: Vertical Reinforcement
stages.narration: Stage 8. Steel bars are inserted through hollow block cores and anchored into the plinth beam.
stages.explanation: Vertical reinforcement improves earthquake resistance and wall stability.

stages.title: Wall Construction
stages.narration: Stage 9. Interlocking blocks are placed course by course. Interlocking action improves load transfer.
stages.explanation: Interlocking blocks improve load transfer while reducing mortar usage.

stages.title: Grouting of Reinforced Cells
stages.narration: Stage 10. Grout is now being poured into reinforced block cores. These reinforced zones significantly increase strength and earthquake resistance.
stages.explanation: Grouted cells create reinforced masonry zones capable of resisting seismic forces.

stages.title: Window and Door Openings
stages.narration: Stage 11. Door and window frames are installed. Reinforced jambs strengthen interrupted load paths.
stages.explanation: Openings require strengthening because they interrupt structural load paths.

stages.title: Lintel Band
stages.narration: Stage 12. The reinforced concrete lintel band is cast continuously around the structure.
stages.explanation: The lintel band ties walls together and prevents separation during earthquakes.

stages.title: Roof Band
stages.narration: Stage 13. Roof-level reinforcement is cast to complete the ring beam.
stages.explanation: The roof band creates box action behavior and improves lateral stability.

stages.title: Roof Construction
stages.narration: Stage 14. Roof formwork, reinforcement and concrete complete the structural diaphragm.
stages.explanation: The roof transfers loads uniformly into supporting walls.

stages.title: Finishing Works
stages.narration: Stage 15. Waterproofing, doors, windows and finishes improve durability and weather resistance.
stages.explanation: Finishing improves durability and weather resistance.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Construction complete. Interlocking reinforced masonry provides improved structural continuity and seismic resistance.
stages.explanation: Completed advanced interlocking brick masonry house ready for occupancy.

interlocking_block.title: Interlocking Hollow Block
interlocking_block.geometry: Mechanical keys align courses and reduce mortar dependence.
interlocking_block.loadTransfer: Compression transferred through interlock keys and grouted cores.
interlocking_block.materialEfficiency: Hollow cores reduce weight; minimal mortar joints.

vertical_reinforcement.title: Vertical Reinforcement
vertical_reinforcement.seismicResistance: Provides ductility and ties walls to plinth and bands.
vertical_reinforcement.anchorage: L-bar into plinth beam with adequate development length.

grouted_core.title: Grouted Reinforcement Cell
grouted_core.structuralStrength: Concrete-grouted core with bar creates reinforced pier.
grouted_core.loadTransfer: Transfers lateral and vertical loads to foundation.

plinth_band.title: Plinth Band
plinth_band.settlementControl: Continuous RC band reduces differential settlement.
plinth_band.continuity: Ties wall bases and anchors vertical reinforcement.

lintel_band.title: Lintel Band
lintel_band.earthquakeResistance: Horizontal tie prevents wall separation under shaking.

roof_band.title: Roof Band
roof_band.boxAction: Ring beam with lintel band creates box action.
roof_band.lateralStability: Walls act together under seismic inertia forces.
